text.title: Freelance Weekly Invoice Cadence Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Freelance Weekly Invoice Cadence methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Invoice batch runs on a fixed weekday (e.g. Monday 09:00 local) every calendar week the engagement is active; skipping a week silently is forbidden.
rule.rationale: predictability is the whole point — clients reschedule cash around your cadence.

rule.statement: Every active client produces exactly one invoice row per cycle even if amount is zero; zero-rows MUST carry an explicit reason (paused, refund, free-month) instead of being silently omitted.
rule.rationale: silently empty client rows make 'we forgot' indistinguishable from 'on purpose'.

rule.statement: Reminder ladder fires at exactly day +3, +7, +14 past the due date with escalating tone (gentle nudge -> firm follow-up -> pause-engagement notice); ad-hoc reminders outside the ladder are forbidden.
rule.rationale: predictable cadence with three rungs collects most without burning the relationship.

rule.statement: Escalation to pause-engagement requires a single named owner on the operator side AND a single named recipient on the client side; generic mailboxes alone are forbidden.
rule.rationale: un-named recipients drop escalations; one human on each side keeps it actionable.

rule.statement: Every non-zero invoice amount MUST cite a verbatim source: timesheet row, scoped deliverable line, or signed retainer clause; fields without anchor are rejected as fabrication.
rule.rationale: LLM-driven invoicing cheerfully invents plausible hours; anchoring keeps it auditable.

rule.statement: Status approved_to_send MUST come from a human reviewer, never the generating agent itself.
rule.rationale: agent self-approval defeats the human checkpoint and the audit trail.
